title: Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: Every proper hereditary class of graphs has the Erdős-Hajnal property.
context_text: A hereditary class $\mathcal{C}$ has the Erdős-Hajnal property if there exists $c>0$ such that every graph $G\in\mathcal{C}$ has a clique or stable set of size at least $|G|^{c}$. This is one of the central open problems in graph theory, with surveys in MR1425208 and MR3150572.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for the special case of all proper hereditary classes of bounded VC-dimension (Theorem 1.2), and for all two-colourable tournaments in the tournament setting (Theorem 6.3).
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension

title: Fox-Pach-Suk Conjecture
statement_text: For every $d>0$, there exists $c>0$ such that every $n$-vertex graph of VC-dimension at most $d$ has a clique or stable set of size at least $n^{c}$.
context_text: Fox, Pach, and Suk came close to settling this in their 2019 paper by proving a bound of $2^{(\log n)^{1-o(1)}}$, where the constant depending on the VC-dimension is hidden in the $o(1)$ term. The conjecture is a special case of the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture for the hereditary class of graphs of bounded VC-dimension.
paper_contribution: Fully confirms the conjecture, proving that every graph $G$ of VC-dimension at most $d$ contains a clique or stable set of size at least $|G|^{c_d}$ for an explicit $c_d>0$ satisfying $c_d \geq 2^{-2^{O(d)}}$.
notes: Stated without a separate labelled conjecture environment; attributed explicitly in the abstract and introduction with cite MR3943496.
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension

title: Chernikov-Starchenko-Thomas Conjecture
statement_text: Every graph definable in NIP structures has a clique or anti-clique of polynomial size.
context_text: In the language of model theory, NIP (Not the Independence Property) structures generalise many natural algebraic examples. Chernikov, Starchenko, and Thomas conjectured a polynomial Erdős-Hajnal-type bound for graphs definable in such structures, extending the classical Erdős-Hajnal problem to a model-theoretic setting.
paper_contribution: Settles the conjecture as a corollary of the main result (Theorem 1.2), since graphs definable in NIP structures have bounded VC-dimension.
notes: Stated without a separate labelled conjecture environment; attributed explicitly in the abstract with cite corresponding to reference [12].
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension

title: Polynomial Rödl Property Conjecture (Conjecture 1.4)
statement_text: Every proper hereditary class of graphs has the polynomial Rödl property.
context_text: Rödl's 1986 theorem showed that for any proper hereditary class there exists $\delta(\varepsilon)>0$ guaranteeing an $\varepsilon$-dense induced bipartite subgraph; Fox and Sudakov conjectured the dependence can be taken polynomial in $\varepsilon$ (i.e.~$\delta = \varepsilon^{c}$ for some $c>0$). This polynomial Rödl property would imply the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture, and the equivalence with related conjectures was further studied in subsequent work.
paper_contribution: Proves Conjecture 1.4 for every hereditary class of graphs of bounded VC-dimension, as a consequence of the main result (Theorem 1.5).
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension

title: Tournament Erdős-Hajnal Conjecture (Conjecture 6.1)
statement_text: Every tournament has the Erdős-Hajnal property.
context_text: A tournament $Q$ has the Erdős-Hajnal property if there exists $c>0$ such that every $n$-vertex $Q$-free tournament admits a transitive subtournament with at least $n^{c}$ vertices. Alon, Pach, and Solymosi proved that this statement is equivalent to the Erdős-Hajnal conjecture (Conjecture 1.1), making it a central reformulation of that problem.
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for every two-colourable tournament, which in particular completes the verification of the conjecture for all six-vertex tournaments.
notes: This statement is equivalent to Conjecture 1.1 (Erdős-Hajnal conjecture) as proved by Alon, Pach, and Solymosi (2001, MR1832443).
paper_title: Induced subgraph density. VI. Bounded VC-dimension
